node1.question: Is the public accessibility of the asset the equipment's intended functionality?

node2.question: Do the physical or logical measures in the targeted operational environment limit the accessibility to authorized entities?

node3.question: Do legal implications not allow access control mechanisms?

node4.question: Are there access control mechanisms that manage entities' access to the security assets and network assets?

node1.question: Do the access control mechanisms ensure that only authorized entities have access to the protected security asset or network asset?

title: Applicability of authentication mechanisms - network interface

node1.question: Is the managed access for network functions or network functions configuration where the absence of authentication is required for the equipment's intended functionality?

node2.question: Is the managed access performed over networks where access is limited to authorized entities?

node3.question: Does the managed access use authentication mechanisms?

title: Applicability of authentication mechanisms - user interface

node1.question: Is the managed access performed over a user interface where physical or logical measures in the targeted environment provide confidence in the correctness of an entity's claim?

node2.question: Is the managed access only for reading of network functions or network functions configuration where access without authentication is needed to enable the intended equipment functionality?

node3.question: Is the managed access only for reading of network functions or network functions configuration where access without authentication is needed because legal implications do not allow for authentication mechanisms?

node4.question: Does the managed access use authentication mechanisms?

node1.question: Does the authentication mechanism examine evidence from at least one element of the categories knowledge, possession and inherence (one factor authentication)?
Does the authentication mechanism examine evidence from at least one element of the categories knowledge, possession and inherence (one factor authentication)?
Does the authentication mechanism validate all relevant properties considering the available information about the authenticator in the operational environments of use?
Does the authentication mechanism validate all relevant properties considering the available information about the authenticator in the operational environments of use?
Does the change of the authenticator conflict security goals?

node2.question: Does the authentication mechanism allow the change of the authenticator?

node1.question: Does the change of the authenticator conflict security goals?

node2.question: Does the authentication mechanism allow the change of the authenticator?

title: Password strength - factory default passwords - network interface

node1.question: Is the password enforced to be changed by the user before or on first use?

node2.question: Is the password unique per equipment?

node3.question: Does the password follow best practice concerning strength?

title: Password strength - factory default passwords - user interface

node1.question: Is the password enforced to be changed by the user before or on first use?

node2.question: Is the password unique per equipment?

node3.question: Does the password follow best practice concerning strength?

title: Password strength - non-factory default passwords - network interface

node1.question: Is the password enforced to be set by the user before or on first use and before the equipment is logically connected to a network?

node2.question: Is the password defined by an authorized entity within a network where access is limited to authorised entities?

node3.question: Is the password generated by the equipment using best practice concerning strength and only communicated to an authorized entity within a network where access is limited to authorised entities?

title: Password strength - non-factory default passwords - user interface

node1.question: Is the password enforced to be set by the user before or on first use and before the equipment is logically connected to a network?

node2.question: Is the password defined by an authorized entity within a network where access is limited to authorised entities?

node3.question: Is the password generated by the equipment using best practice concerning strength and only communicated to an authorized entity within a network where access is limited to authorised entities?

node1.question: Has the authentication mechanism the capability to be resilient against brute force attacks?
Has the authentication mechanism the capability to be resilient against brute force attacks?
